pattern: With such a small upper bound, the fastest path to a correct answer is simply to compute every digit sum from one up to that bound and tally the even ones directly.
hints: The bound is small enough that checking every integer from one up to it individually is fast enough.
For each integer, break it down digit by digit and add them up to get its digit sum.
Only tally the integer if that digit sum comes out even.

edge_inputs.description: smallest possible input, digit sum is odd
smallest input with an even digit sum
a two-digit number whose digits sum to an even total
a value right at the single-to-double-digit boundary
a value right at the double-to-triple-digit boundary

stress.description: random values across the full bound
